Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We need to construct a DECLARE model as a Python dictionary with keys for each constraint type The scenario describes a product design and launch process We need to define constraints that capture typical ordering co occurrence etc The model should be realistic based on the activities

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We have activities IG DD TFC CE PC LT UT AG MP FL

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Constraints We need to define for each key the appropriate rules For unary constraints existence absence exactly_one init we specify activities and support confidence For binary constraints we specify pairs

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: But careful For binary constraints like response the key is activity A and the value is another dict Actually description says for binary constraints value is a dictionary with keys as activities and corresponding value support and confidence But careful a rule involves two activities

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: I think the safest is to assume that for binary constraints the keys are tuples act1 act2 mapping to support confidence

Name: Validation - PE
Text: That makes sense So we will do that For unary key is a single string activity name mapping to support confidence For binary key is a tuple act1 act2 mapping to support confidence

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: We can set confidence to 1 point 0 for all to simplify The support is given as 1 point 0 so we will use that

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Lets think about typical process flow The process might start with IG Then DD then TFC and CE might be parallel After design draft technical feasibility and cost evaluation are done Then prototype creation then lab testing and user testing then approval gate then marketing plan then final launch

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Unary existence at least once for IG AG FL absence maybe none exactly_one FL should happen exactly once init IG is the init first activity

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: We can define succession constraints IG DD DD TFC DD CE TFC PC CE PC PC LT PC UT LT AG UT AG AG MP MP FL

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: TFC and CE might be coexistence They both need to happen after DD So we could have coexistence TFC CE Also for testing LT UT might be both done

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: We will use chainresponse for strict ordering like AG MP and MP FL

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: We will also have nonsuccession For example FL should never be before IG so FL IG nonsuccession

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Given the open ended nature we will produce a reasonable set

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We need to output just the Python dictionary We will format it nicely
